message:
feat(indexer/semantic): land semantic backfill scheduler decision types

Introduce the decision types and pure policy function that govern
whether a background semantic backfill pass should run right now,
integrating the responsiveness governor's capacity signal with a
set of environment and policy signals. The actual scheduler loop
that consumes these decisions lands in a follow-up slice; this
commit reserves the vocabulary so the loop can compose cleanly
against it.

New internal types (all `pub(crate)` — crate-internal surface):
- `SemanticBackfillSchedulerState`:
  `Running | Paused | Disabled` with a `.as_str()` that serializes
  as snake_case. Paused is for transient yields (e.g. foreground
  pressure, lexical repair in progress) and Disabled is for
  explicit policy/operator-off states.
- `SemanticBackfillSchedulerReason`:
  `IdleBudgetAvailable`, `OperatorDisabled` (env flag), `PolicyDisabled`
  (SemanticPolicy.mode says no), `ForegroundPressure`
  (index-under-interactive-load), `LexicalRepairActive`
  (lexical rebuild is running and should claim all budget),
  `CapacityBelowFloor` (governor-reported capacity below the
  configurable floor), `ThreadBudgetZero`, `BatchBudgetZero`
  (nothing to schedule). Each has a `.next_step()` human-readable
  hint so `cass diag` / `cass doctor` surfaces can render the
  reason without re-deriving it.
- `SemanticBackfillSchedulerSignals`:
  The opaque set of truthy env bits that bias the decision
  (`CASS_SEMANTIC_BACKFILL_{FOREGROUND_ACTIVE,LEXICAL_REPAIR_ACTIVE,
  FORCE,DISABLE}`), collected via `from_env()`. `force` opts out of
  every pause/disable branch *except* `BatchBudgetZero`, matching
  the "emergency, i'll babysit it" semantics.
- `SemanticBackfillSchedulerDecision`:
  The full decision record — state, reason, requested vs.
  scheduled batch size, capacity readings, thread/batch budgets,
  the foreground/repair/force booleans that fed the decision, and
  the `next_eligible_after_ms` back-off hint. Has `should_run()`
  convenience.

New policy function:
- `semantic_backfill_scheduler_decision(policy, requested, &signals)`
  routes through `semantic_backfill_scheduler_decision_for_capacity`
  with the live `responsiveness::current_capacity_pct()`; the
  `_for_capacity` variant is pure (takes capacity as an argument)
  so unit tests can pin every branch deterministically.
  Decision order: zero-batch → zero-threads (unless forced) →
  operator-disabled (unless forced) → policy-mode off (unless
  forced) → lexical repair active (pause) → foreground pressure
  (pause) → capacity below floor (pause) → Running with
  scaled `scheduled_batch_conversations =
  max(1, min(requested, requested * capacity / 100))`.
- `stopped_scheduler_decision` centralizes the "zero out the
  batch and stamp state/reason/next_eligible_after_ms" shape.

Support:
- `env_truthy(key)` tolerates `1 / true / yes / on` (trimmed,
  case-insensitive). `env_backfill_min_capacity_pct()` reads
  `CASS_SEMANTIC_BACKFILL_MIN_CAPACITY_PCT` (default 75, clamped
  1..=100) so operators can tighten or loosen the capacity floor
  without touching code.

No call sites yet; the scheduler loop wires these in the follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
